steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Central Ideas and Details question asking specifically what Sun and colleagues discovered about surfactants. The passage states that surfactants “have a much stronger wave-reducing effect” than microplastic particle concentration alone could explain.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘They can be used to contain microplastics within certain areas of the ocean.’: The passage never mentions containing or confining microplastics; surfactants are discussed only in terms of wave reduction. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘They are mainly composed of water.’: The passage describes surfactants as chemicals released from plastics; no information about their chemical composition is given. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘They have a much stronger effect on wave activity than microplastics alone do.’: This restates the passage’s direct finding that surfactants have “a much stronger wave-reducing effect” than microplastic particle concentration alone. ✓
  • Choice D — ‘They are helpful for removing microplastics from the ocean.’: The passage says nothing about removing microplastics; surfactants are a byproduct of plastics, not a cleanup agent. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
